title: Jabil Invests in the Future of AI with Intel Silicon Photonics Transceiver Deal
text: ST. PETERSBURG, Fla.--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Jabil Inc. (NYSE: JBL), a global leader in design, manufacturing, and supply chain solutions, today announced it will take over the manufacture and sale of Intel's current Silicon Photonics-based pluggable optical transceiver (“module”) product lines and the development of future generations of such modules. title: Jabil: Sustained Growth And Expanding Margins Make Me Bullish
text: Jabil Inc. has been successful in expanding its market share and revenue through key partnerships with companies like Tesla, Johnson & Johnson, and Amazon Web Services. The company's long-term guidance exceeds investor expectations, with positive outlooks for higher-growth and higher-margin segments. Jabil's sale of its mobility business to BYD Electronic will enhance its portfolio and financial flexibility, while its healthcare division remains a key driver of revenue and growth.

title: Jabil: Despite The Rip Higher, More Is Yet To Come
text: JBL stock is up 123% over the past year. While investors may be tempted to take profits, I say "not so fast". The $2.2 billion divestiture of its (low-margin) China Mobility segment to BYD is going to enable JBL to accelerate its stock buyback plan to invest in higher-margin higher-growth businesses. The current $2.5 billion share buyback authorization equates to ~14% of the company's current market-cap.

title: Fictiv Launches Major USA 3D Printing Service Expansion
